canonical_value:
A 3-phase, 440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.

The motor is coupled to a 220 V, separately excited D.C. generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \). Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the DC generator power output?

Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the DC generator power output?

normalized_value.target_text: If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?

canonical_value:
In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators. The output of the comparators is then encoded to a binary format using digital circuits. Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF. The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion. Assume that the time taken by the thermometer-to-binary encoder is negligible.

If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?

canonical_value: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. The transient reactance of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu. One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when δ(t) = 130° as shown in the image. What is the maximum value of the per unit line reactance such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping?

canonical_value:
The figure illustrates a cascaded system of two water tanks. Water flows:
- into the first tank at a rate \( r_0(t) \),
- out of the first tank and into the second at a rate \( r_1(t) \), and
- out of the second tank at a rate \( r_2(t) \).
The rate of flow out of each tank is proportional to the height of the water in that tank: \( r_1(t) = k_1 h_1(t) \) and \( r_2(t) = k_2 h_2(t) \), where \( k_1 \) and \( k_2 \) are each \( 0.2 \, \text{m}^2/\text{second} \). Both tanks have heights of 1 m. The cross-sectional area of tank 1 is \( A_1 = 4 \, \text{m}^2 \) and that of the second tank is \( A_2 = 2 \, \text{m}^2 \). At time \( t = 0 \), both tanks are empty. Assume that \( r_0(t) \) is held constant at rate \( r_0 \).
What is the maximum value of \( r_0 \) such that neither tank will ever overflow if both tanks start out empty?
\( r_0 = \) ________ m\(^3\)/s

canonical_value:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?

\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?

normalized_value.title: EEE-Bench 子题 2
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?

normalized_value.text: In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):
S_{PM}(t) = \cos(1000\pi t + K_p m(t))
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)

canonical_value: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

normalized_value.target_text: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

canonical_value:
In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

\[
S_{PM}(t) = \cos(1000\pi t + K_p m(t))
\]
and
\[
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)
\]

where \( K_p \) is the phase deviation constant in radians/volt and \( K_f \) is the frequency deviation constant in radians/second/volt. If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.
surface_forms:

\]
and
\[
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)
\]

where \( K_p \) is the phase deviation constant in radians/volt and \( K_f \) is the frequency deviation constant in radians/second/volt. If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.
